messages.content:
I have a vba code that highlights the row of an active cell. Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
Dim activeRow As Integer 'Declare a variable to hold the active row number
Cells.Interior.ColorIndex = 0 'Reset all cells to no color
Target.EntireRow.Interior.ColorIndex = 36 'Highlight active row
End Sub

As an AI language model, I can see that the given code will highlight the row of an active cell with color index 36. Here is a breakdown of what each line of the code does:

- Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range): Defines a Worksheet_SelectionChange event-triggered subroutine that activates when the user changes the selection within the worksheet.
- Dim activeRow As Integer: Declares a variable named activeRow and sets its data type to Integer. This variable is not used in the code, but it could be used to store the row number of the active cell if needed.
- Cells.Interior.ColorIndex = 0: Resets the color index of all cells in the worksheet to 0 (which means no color).
- Target.EntireRow.Interior.ColorIndex = 36: Sets the color index of the entire row containing the active cell (which is represented by the Target range variable) to 36, which is a teal shade.

Overall, this code is a useful tool for quickly highlighting the active row in a worksheet, making it easier to focus on the data in that row.
I want to select another sheet named List2 and for the same row of the last active row in sheet List to also be highlighted

Sure, here's an updated version of the code to highlight the same row in Sheet "List2" as the last active row in Sheet "List1":

```
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim activeRow As Integer 'Declare a variable to hold the active row number
    activeRow = Target.Row 'Store the row number of the active cell in activeRow variable
    Cells.Interior.ColorIndex = 0 'Reset all cells to no color
    
    'Highlight active row in current sheet
    Target.EntireRow.Interior.ColorIndex = 36 
    
    'Highlight same row in List2 sheet
    Sheets("List2").Range("A" & activeRow).EntireRow.Interior.ColorIndex = 36 
End Sub
```

The updated version of the code first stores the row number of the active cell in the activeRow variable. It then highlights the active row in the current sheet and uses the activeRow variable to highlight the same row in Sheet "List2". 

Note that the code assumes both sheets have the same number of rows and start from the same row. If this is not the case, you'll need to adjust the code accordingly.

how can i use the following, Dim activeRow As Integer 'Declare a variable to hold the active row number
activeRow = Target.Row 'Store the row number of the active cell in activeRow variable, to automatically highlight the row in a new active sheet

If you want to use the `activeRow` variable to highlight the row in a new active sheet, you can modify the code like this:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim activeRow As Integer 'Declare a variable to hold the active row number
    activeRow = Target.Row 'Store the row number of the active cell in activeRow variable
    Cells.Interior.ColorIndex = 0 'Reset all cells to no color
    
    'Highlight active row in current sheet
    Target.EntireRow.Interior.ColorIndex = 36 
    
    'Highlight same row in new active sheet
    If TypeName(Application.ActiveSheet) = "Worksheet" Then
        'If the active sheet is a worksheet, highlight the same row in a new active sheet
        Sheets(Application.ActiveSheet.Name).Activate
        If Sheets(Application.ActiveSheet.Name).Index = Sheets.Count Then
            'If the active sheet is the last sheet, go to the first sheet
            Sheets(1).Activate
        Else
            'Otherwise, go to the next sheet
            Sheets(Application.ActiveSheet.Index + 1).Activate
        End If
        Sheets(Application.ActiveSheet.Name).Range("A" & activeRow).EntireRow.Interior.ColorIndex = 36
    End If
End Sub
```

This version of the code first checks if the active sheet is a worksheet, to avoid any issues if the user is on a chart or other object. If the active sheet is a worksheet, it activates the same sheet to get to the next sheet. If the active sheet is the last sheet in the workbook, it jumps to the first sheet. Finally, it uses the `activeRow` variable to highlight the same row in the new active sheet.

Note that this code assumes that you are using the same sheet structure in all sheets, otherwise you'll need to adjust the code further.
what is the vba code that responds to an an active sheet named List2

If you want to respond to an active sheet named "List2", you can use the following code:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Application.ActiveSheet.Name = "List2" Then
        'Your code here for List2 sheet
    End If
End Sub
```

This code checks if the active sheet is named "List2". If it is, it runs the code between the `If` and `End If` statements. You can put any relevant code for the "List2" sheet there.

Note that this code should typically be placed in the module for the sheet(s) you want it to apply to, not in a standard module.
i want to use work sheet open

If you want to run a code when a specific sheet is opened, you can use the `Worksheet_Activate` event. Here's an example of the code you can use for the "List2" sheet:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Activate()
    If ActiveSheet.Name = "List2" Then
        'Your code here for List2 sheet
    End If
End Sub
```

This code checks if the current active sheet is named "List2" and runs the code between the `If` and `End If` statements if it is. You can put any relevant code for the "List2" sheet there.

Note that this code should be placed in the module for the "List2" sheet.

is there a vba code that starts Private Sub Worksheet Open

Yes, you can use the `Worksheet_Open` event to run a VBA code when a specific worksheet is opened. Here's an example of the code you can use for the "List2" sheet:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Open()
    'Your code here for the "List2" sheet
End Sub
```

This code runs when the "List2" sheet is opened and executes any VBA code placed in it. You can put any relevant code for the "List2" sheet within this subroutine.

Note that this code should be placed in the module for the "List2" sheet.
I want to open sheet named List2 and automatically select the same cell that was active in  previous sheet named List

Here's an example code that opens the "List2" sheet and selects the same cell that was active in the previous sheet ("List1" in this example):

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Activate()
    Dim previousSheet As Worksheet
    Dim activeCell As Range
    
    Set previousSheet = Worksheets("List1") 'Change the sheet name to the previous sheet name
    Set activeCell = previousSheet.Range(ActiveCell.Address)
    
    'Activate List2 sheet
    Worksheets("List2").Activate
    
    'Select the same cell that was active in the previous sheet
    activeCell.Select
End Sub
```

This code uses two variables to store the previous sheet and the active cell range in that sheet. It then activates the "List2" sheet and selects the same cell that was active in the previous sheet.

Note that this code should be placed in the module for the "List2" sheet. Also, if the previous sheet was not named "List1", you'll need to update the sheet name in the code accordingly.
I want a vba code that when “List2” sheet is opened the same cell that was active in the previous sheet “List1” is selected

Here's an example code that opens the "List2" sheet and selects the same cell that was active in the previous sheet ("List1" in this example):

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Activate()
    Dim previousSheet As Worksheet
    Dim activeCell As Range
    
    Set previousSheet = Worksheets("List1") 'Change the sheet name to the previous sheet name
    Set activeCell = previousSheet.Range(ActiveCell.Address)
    
    'Activate List2 sheet
    Worksheets("List2").Activate

    activeCell.Select
End Sub
```

This code uses two variables to store the previous sheet and the active cell range in that sheet. It then activates the "List2" sheet and selects the same cell that was active in the previous sheet.

Note that this code should be placed in the module for the "List2" sheet. Also, if the previous sheet was not named "List1", you'll need to update the sheet name in the code accordingly.
how can i store the address of an active cell to an integer and when i open another sheet use the stored value to select the cell of the same address

If you want to store the address of an active cell as an integer and use the stored value to select the cell of the same address when you open another sheet, you can use the following code:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim activeRow As Integer
    Dim activeCol As Integer
    Static previousSheetName As String
    Static previousAddress As Integer
    
    If Not Target.Address = previousAddress Or Not Application.ActiveSheet.Name = previousSheetName Then
        'Store the address of the active cell and the sheet name if it is different
        activeRow = Target.Row
        activeCol = Target.Column
        previousAddress = Target.Address
        previousSheetName = Application.ActiveSheet.Name
    End If
End Sub

Private Sub Worksheet_Activate()
    Dim previousSheet As Worksheet
    Dim activeCell As Range
    
    Set previousSheet = Worksheets(previousSheetName)
    Set activeCell = previousSheet.Cells(activeRow, activeCol)
    
    'Activate List2 sheet
    Worksheets("List2").Activate
    
    'Select the same cell that was active in the previous sheet
    activeCell.Select
End Sub
```

This code uses two `Static` variables to store the previous sheet name and active cell address. When the `Worksheet_SelectionChange` event is triggered, the code stores the row and column of the active cell and the sheet name (if it is different from the previous sheet name) in the variables. 

When the "List2" sheet is activated, the code uses the stored sheet name and cell row and column to activate the previous sheet and select the same cell that was active.

Note that this code should be placed in the module for the "List2" sheet.
If want to store the address of an active cell as an integer . When I open a different sheet, i want to use the stored value from the previous sheet to select the cell of the same address

You can store the address of an active cell as an integer using the `Row` and `Column` properties of the `Range` object. Here's an example of how you can store and retrieve the active cell address:

```
Dim activeRow As Long 'Declare a variable to hold the row number
Dim activeCol As Long 'Declare a variable to hold the column number

Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    activeRow = Target.Row 'Store the row number of the active cell in activeRow variable
    activeCol = Target.Column 'Store the column number of the active cell in activeCol variable
End Sub

Private Sub Workbook_SheetActivate(ByVal Sh As Object)
    If Sh.Name = "List2" Then 'Change "List2" to the name of the sheet you want to select the cell in
        Sh.Cells(activeRow, activeCol).Select 'Select the cell with the same row and column as the last active cell from the previous sheet
    End If
End Sub
```

This code stores the row number of the active cell in the `activeRow` variable and the column number of the active cell in the `activeCol` variable. When the "List2" sheet is activated, the code checks if the name of the sheet is "List2" and selects the cell with the same row and column as the last active cell from the previous sheet.

Note that this code should be placed in the `ThisWorkbook` module of your VBA project. The `Workbook_SheetActivate` event is triggered whenever a sheet is activated, so it will work for any sheet you want to apply this functionality to.
I am getting an error with this line of code, Cells(activeRow, activeCol).Select 'Select the cell with the same row and column as the last active cell from the previous sheet

If you are getting an error with the line `Cells(activeRow, activeCol).Select` when using it in the `Workbook_SheetActivate` event, it may be because the `activeRow` and `activeCol` variables are not declared as global variables at the module level, and therefore are not accessible from the `Workbook_SheetActivate` event.

To fix this, you can declare `activeRow` and `activeCol` as global variables by placing their declarations outside of any Sub or Function in the module, like this:

```
Dim activeRow As Long 'Declare a variable to hold the row number as a global variable
Dim activeCol As Long 'Declare a variable to hold the column number as a global variable

Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    activeRow = Target.Row 'Store the row number of the active cell in activeRow variable
    activeCol = Target.Column 'Store the column number of the active cell in activeCol variable
End Sub

Private Sub Workbook_SheetActivate(ByVal Sh As Object)
    If Sh.Name = "List2" Then 'Change "List2" to the name of the sheet you want to select the cell in
        Cells(activeRow, activeCol).Select 'Select the cell with the same row and column as the last active cell from the previous sheet
    End If
End Sub
```

By declaring `activeRow` and `activeCol` as global variables, they can be accessed from any Sub or Function in the module, including the `Workbook_SheetActivate` event, allowing you to select the cell with the same row and column as the last active cell from the previous sheet.
activeRow and activeColumn are variables that were stored in a previous sheet. how can i use these stored variables to select the intersection cell in a new sheet that i have activated

If you have previously stored the row and column of the active cell in `activeRow` and `activeColumn` variables on one sheet, and you want to use these stored variables to select the intersection cell on another sheet that has been activated, you can use a code like this:

```
Sub SelectIntersectionCell()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim intersectionCell As Range
    Dim activeRow As Long
    Dim activeColumn As Long
    
    'Get the previously stored values of activeRow and activeColumn variables from the previous sheet
    activeRow = Sheets("PreviousSheet").activeRow
    activeColumn = Sheets("PreviousSheet").activeColumn
    
    'Activate the new sheet
    Set ws = Worksheets("NewSheet")
    ws.Activate
    
    'Select the cell at the intersection of activeRow and activeColumn in the new sheet
    Set intersectionCell = ws.Cells(activeRow, activeColumn)
    intersectionCell.Select
End Sub
```

This code first gets the previously stored values of `activeRow` and `activeColumn` variables from the previous sheet (in this example, we assume that the variables were stored in a sheet named "PreviousSheet"). It then activates the new sheet (in this example, we assume that the sheet to activate is named "NewSheet"). Finally, it selects the cell at the intersection of `activeRow` and `activeColumn` in the new sheet using the `Cells` property.

Note that you will need to modify this code to match your specific case. Specifically, you will need to change:

- `"PreviousSheet"` to the name of the sheet where the variables were stored
- `"NewSheet"` to the name of the sheet that has been activated

If you have any further questions, please let me know!
